title: Parent DB verification and regression hardening
summary: Verify that parent DB models, metadata, migrations, enums, repo/database wiring, and docker test topology are correct and resistant to future regressions after the model-layout refactor.

items.description: Inventory the current verification surface for the parent DB layer and list what is already covered versus missing after the model-file split landed.
steps: Search for existing DB model, metadata, migration, and repo tests.
List what is already covered and what still needs verification, distinguishing SQLite-backed unit coverage from Postgres/docker coverage.
Record the discovery summary in progress/progress.txt.
pass_criteria: A clear test-gap inventory exists in the progress log.

items.description: Add migration/bootstrap verification proving that the parent DB can be migrated cleanly in the containerized test topology and that failures point at metadata or revision drift.
steps: Add a test or integration check that exercises migration/bootstrap behavior in the test environment against the real Postgres-backed compose topology.
Ensure failures are readable when metadata and migration state drift.
pass_criteria: Migration/bootstrap verification exists and passes in docker.

items.description: Add a dedicated metadata/table registration verification file for the full parent DB surface.
steps: Assert the expected parent tables are present in SQLAlchemy metadata.
Group assertions by domain so failures are understandable.
pass_criteria: The test proves the expected parent tables are registered.

items.description: Add enum/vocabulary verification tests for locked parent-side vocabularies.
steps: Verify the implemented enum/choice sets match the locked decision record.

Focus on account status, roles, support statuses, announcement severity/scope, service status, address types, integration status, and projection status fields that are already represented in the model layer.
pass_criteria: Vocabulary tests would fail if code drifts from the locked decision record.

items.description: Add Postgres-backed ORM/session round-trip tests for key parent-owned domains.
steps: Add focused integration tests for representative CRUD/read-model behavior across identity, addresses, support, and Pay-derived projections using `SessionLocal` and the migrated Postgres schema.
pass_criteria: Representative parent-owned database round-trip tests exist and pass.

items.description: Add Postgres-backed uniqueness/index/constraint verification where it matters for parent-owned tables and projection tables.
steps: Verify key uniqueness and constraint behavior such as immutable/unique usernames, address primacy assumptions if enforced, support ticket code uniqueness, and projection-table uniqueness where applicable.
pass_criteria: Constraint/uniqueness behavior is covered by integration tests.

items.description: Add docker-topology verification tests/checks ensuring the authoritative test path remains valid for this repo.
steps: Verify the test topology files exist and match the expected repo contract, including the `db` -> `migrate` -> `test` dependency path in `docker-compose.test.yml`.
Add a light regression check for required services/commands if that can be done robustly.
pass_criteria: The repo has explicit regression coverage or validation around the authoritative test topology.

items.description: Run the authoritative docker test suite and mark the spec complete only after the full suite is green.
steps: Only then mark the spec complete.
pass_criteria: The authoritative docker suite exits green.
All prior items in this spec are complete.
